retrieved_result: Kafka is written in scala. Why does the API described in the official documentation is the Java's one? Gwen Shapira, Apache Kafka Committer, Works for Confluent. Answered Sep 3, 2015 · Upvoted by Umesh Chaudhary, Performance Engineer of Apache Kafka Apache Kafka is in transition from the Scala APIs to the Java ones. Reason is that Scala doesn't maintain binary compatibility between versions, so in or
Decision_Reason: The retrieved result discusses Kafka's Java vs Scala APIs and general API compatibility, not guidance on debugging API integration, so it does not address the user's query.
